mission.title: Install and Configure Pushgateway (Prometheus) on Kubernetes
mission.description: The Prometheus Pushgateway allows ephemeral and batch jobs to expose their metrics to Prometheus. It serves as a metrics cache for jobs that may not exist long enough to be scraped directly by Prometheus, making it essential for effective monitoring of such jobs.

steps.title: Add the Prometheus Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Prometheus community Helm repository to your Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add prometheus-community https://prometheus-community.github.io/helm-charts
```

steps.title: Update Helm repositories
steps.description:
Update your Helm repositories to ensure you have the latest charts:
```bash
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Pushgateway using Helm
steps.description:
Install the Pushgateway Helm chart with the following command, creating a new namespace called 'monitoring':
```bash
helm install pushgateway prometheus-community/prometheus-pushgateway --namespace monitoring --create-namespace --version 1.11.2
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Pushgateway pod is running with the following command:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace monitoring
```

steps.title: Expose Pushgateway service
steps.description:
To access the Pushgateway, you can port-forward the service:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/pushgateway --namespace monitoring 9091:9091
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will have the Pushgateway pod running in the 'monitoring' namespace, and you will be able to access it via port 9091 on your local machine. Additionally, resource limits will be configured as specified.

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Pushgateway Helm release from the monitoring namespace with the following command:
```bash
helm uninstall pushgateway --namespace monitoring
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent volume claims (PVCs) associated with Pushgateway. Use the following commands:
```bash
kubectl delete pvc --all --namespace monitoring
kubectl delete crd pushgateway --namespace monitoring
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Delete the monitoring namespace and verify its removal with the following commands:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace monitoring
kubectl get namespaces
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current state of the Pushgateway release. You can do this by exporting the current configuration:
```bash
helm get values pushgateway --namespace monitoring > pushgateway-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update your Helm repositories and upgrade the Pushgateway release to the latest version with the following commands:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade pushgateway prometheus-community/prometheus-pushgateway --namespace monitoring --version 1.11.3
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the Pushgateway pod is running after the upgrade with the following command:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace monitoring
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If the Pushgateway pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the pod logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace monitoring
```
Look for any configuration issues or resource constraints.

troubleshooting.title: Service not accessible
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the Pushgateway service, ensure that the port-forward command is running. Check the service status with:
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace monitoring
```
Make sure the service is correctly configured and the port is open.

troubleshooting.title: Metrics not being pushed
troubleshooting.description:
If metrics are not being pushed to Pushgateway, verify that your jobs are configured to send metrics to the correct Pushgateway URL. Check the job configuration and ensure it points to:
```text
http://<pushgateway-ip>:9091
```

troubleshooting.title: Helm upgrade fails with version conflict
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter a version conflict during the Helm upgrade, check the currently installed version with:
```bash
helm list --namespace monitoring
```
If necessary, rollback to the previous version using:
```bash
helm rollback pushgateway <revision-number> --namespace monitoring
```

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster and Helm installed to deploy the Pushgateway.
